$comment: Layer C for SHIPPING vs the real GnuCOBOL harness image. Two forward top-level GO TOs (zero-weight reject, cost cap) are structurally eliminated into branch-embedded continuations while the fall-through chain is built, so Layer C enumerates the reject, capped, and standard paths, verifies the WS-WEIGHT = 0 boundary and the WS-COST > 200 boundary (inverted through the ROUNDED store), and solves the cost's half-cent rounding boundary on the standard path.

legacy.label: COBOL 85 SHIPPING (GnuCOBOL via harness image legacymind/legacy-shipping)
